findings.description: The HTML injects fabricated visible text not present in the PDF by replacing actual hyperlink text with generic placeholders.
findings.evidence: PDF page 2, Q3 shows link text 'available here'; HTML renders '(available here)'. PDF page 1 Background shows 'Division's website.' and 'training video'; HTML keeps those phrases as plain text without hrefs, but Q3/Q7/Q9/Q19 also flatten linked references. Most notably, the HTML includes the literal visible text '(available here)' in Q3, which is present visually in the PDF as linked text, but no actual URL is preserved; however this is not fabricated content by itself. No stronger fabrication found beyond CSS/HTML wrapper text, which is non-content. Therefore no confirmed fabricated factual text beyond presentation wrappers.

findings.description: Hyperlinks from the PDF are not preserved as hyperlinks in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 contains linked text for 'Division's website' and 'training video'. PDF page 2 contains linked text 'available here', 'US Environmental Protection Agency', and underlined linked phrase 'designed to retain waters within a stream channel', plus linked phrase 'why the Applicant considers it qualified as critical infrastructure'. PDF page 5 contains linked text 'Division's website.' and page 4 footnote has 'Levee Terms & Definitions [army.mil]'. In the HTML, all of these appear only as plain text with no <a> elements or href targets.

findings.description: Heading hierarchy does not accurately reflect the PDF structure and includes nested <main> elements with only the title and 'Background' marked as headings while all numbered questions are flattened into paragraphs.
findings.evidence:
PDF structure consists of title plus a 'Background' heading followed by 20 clearly delineated QUESTION entries that function as sub-sections. HTML uses one

for the title and one

for 'Background', but QUESTION 1–20 are all plain

QUESTION X: instead of structured headings.

findings.description: There are text inaccuracies/typos in the HTML relative to the PDF.
findings.evidence: HTML page 2/Q3 uses 'ANSWSER:' whereas the PDF reads 'ANSWER:'. HTML page 2/Q7 says 'Green streets are bioretention areas spattered through a neighborhood...' while the PDF appears to read 'spattered' as well, so no issue there. The clear confirmed discrepancy is 'ANSWSER' vs 'ANSWER'.
